canonical_question: What are the EU AI Act compliance requirements by risk tier?
aliases: EU AI Act risk tiers explained
EU AI Act prohibited practices list
EU AI Act high-risk AI system requirements
EU AI Act penalties and fines
constraints: EU jurisdiction only with extraterritorial reach for providers placing AI on the EU market
Phased enforcement: prohibited practices (Feb 2025), GPAI (Aug 2025), high-risk (Aug 2026), regulated-product high-risk (Aug 2027)
Harmonised standards still being developed by CEN/CENELEC — compliance assessed against essential requirements directly
SME carve-outs: reduced fines and regulatory sandbox access for SMEs and startups
Interacts with GDPR, Digital Services Act, Product Safety Regulation, Medical Device Regulation, and Machinery Regulation

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User operates exclusively outside the EU and does not place AI systems on the EU market
User is asking specifically about GDPR data protection obligations

inputs_needed.question: Is the organization a provider (developer), deployer (user), importer, or distributor of the AI system?
options: Provider (develops or commissions the AI system)
Deployer (uses the AI system under their authority)
Importer (places third-country AI on EU market)
Distributor (makes AI available on EU market)

inputs_needed.question: What risk category does the AI system fall into?
options: High-risk (Annex I safety component or Annex III listed)

inputs_needed.question: Is the AI system placed on the EU market or used within the EU?

applies_to.user_segment: Organizations developing, deploying, or importing AI systems in or into the EU market
applies_to.context: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 — the world's first comprehensive AI regulation
